title: Create M4 planning package
summary: Create the reviewed M4 planning package from the WM-0060 future M4 entry prompt, including scenario contract, ADR, task DAG, risk/performance/migration plan, and executable M4 task packets without implementing runtime M4 behavior.
acceptance: M4 scenario contract defines a concrete playable rule-discovery path and explicit non-goals
ADR-0009 or equivalent records M4 ownership, evidence facts, crisis state, director boundaries, save/replay scope and Worker projections
M4 task DAG JSON packets are created with dependencies, owners, reviewers, allowed paths, forbidden paths, checks, benchmark impact, rollback model and owner gates
Planning report records startup audit, M3 baseline evidence, M4 objective, risks, performance/invariant plan and migration policy
No runtime M4 implementation begins in WM-0061 and no M5 task is created or implemented

history.details: Created M4 planning package: scenario contract, ADR-0009, roadmap plan, proposed WM-0062 through WM-0071 task DAG, reports and narrow state updates; no runtime M4 implementation and no M5 task; required checks pass.

verified: No blocking findings. Reviewed scenario contract, ADR-0009, roadmap updates, project-state marker, WM-0061 reports and WM-0062 through WM-0071 proposed packets. Required checks passed; no runtime M4 implementation or M5 task detected.

Reviewed WM-0061 planning package accepted; integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check and pnpm quality. Downstream M4 packets remain proposed; no runtime M4 or M5 work started.

objective: Create the reviewed M4 planning package, including scenario contract, ADR, roadmap plan, proposed task DAG and reports, without implementing runtime M4 behavior or starting M5.
benchmarkImpact: None directly. WM-0061 defines required M4 benchmark and invariant tasks while preserving the existing 10 percent warning and 20 percent blocking thresholds.
reviewRouting: Systems-architect completes to reviewer. Project-director integrates only after independent verification that downstream M4 tasks remain proposed and no runtime M4 or M5 work started.
rollbackModel: Revert the M4 planning documents, ADR-0009, proposed WM-0062 through WM-0071 packets, WM-0061 reports and narrow roadmap/project-state markers; restore M3 closed / M4 planning not started.
ownerGate: Do not edit product runtime tests benchmarks package manifests or generated baselines in WM-0061
Keep all downstream M4 tasks proposed until WM-0061 is reviewed integrated and done
Define explicit block conditions for owner-store gaps global scans public protocol save schema dependencies UI authority and M5 scope creep
Preserve M0-M3 regression protection and independent reviewer separation

sparkEligibility.reason: M4 milestone planning, ADR ownership, task DAG design, save/replay scope and benchmark policy cannot be assigned to Spark.

review.summary: No blocking findings. Reviewed scenario contract, ADR-0009, roadmap updates, project-state marker, WM-0061 reports and WM-0062 through WM-0071 proposed packets. Required checks passed; no runtime M4 implementation or M5 task detected.

integration.summary: Reviewed WM-0061 planning package accepted; integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check and pnpm quality. Downstream M4 packets remain proposed; no runtime M4 or M5 work started.
